pairs.question: What was the official name of the 1959 USAC Championship Car season?
pairs.answer: The 1959 USAC Championship Car season was officially called the 1959 USAC National Championship Trail.

pairs.question: Who won the 1959 USAC National Championship and the Indianapolis 500?
pairs.answer: Rodger Ward won both the 1959 USAC National Championship and the Indianapolis 500.

pairs.question: How many fatal accidents occurred during the 1959 USAC Championship Car season?
pairs.answer: Seven fatal accidents occurred during the 1959 USAC Championship Car season.

pairs.question: What happened to Marshall Teague before the start of the 1959 season?
pairs.answer: Marshall Teague was fatally injured in a crash at Daytona during the pre-season. He was 37 years old.

pairs.question: List the names and ages of all drivers who died during the 1959 season.
pairs.answer: The drivers who died during the 1959 season were: Marshall Teague (37), George Amick (34), Dick Linder (36), Jerry Unser (26), Bob Cortner (32), Van Johnson (32), and Ed Elisian (32).

pairs.question: Which race had two fatalities in the 1959 season, and who were the drivers?
pairs.answer: The Indianapolis 500 had two fatalities in the 1959 season. Jerry Unser (26) died on May 2, and Bob Cortner (32) died on May 19, both in practice accidents.

pairs.question: How many non-championship events were there in the 1959 USAC Championship Car season?
pairs.answer: There were three non-championship events in the 1959 USAC Championship Car season.
